description: Complete the authentication screen set and harden the session lifecycle. Builds on MVP 1's auth adapter pattern, secure store wrapper, and logger. Delivers: sign-up, forgot password, OTP verification, reset password screens, session expired modal, account locked screen, and the full better-auth + custom backend adapter implementations.

api.name: signIn
api.description: Authenticate with email and password. Returns token and user. Already partially implemented — move to adapter.

api.name: signUp
api.description: Register a new account with email and password.

api.name: forgotPassword
api.description: Send password reset OTP/link to email.

api.name: verifyOtp
api.description: Verify OTP code sent to email.

api.name: resetPassword
api.description: Reset password using reset token from OTP verification.

api.name: refreshSession
api.description: Refresh access token using refresh token.

api.name: getSession
api.description: Validate current token and return session user. Called on cold start.

auth_adapter_pattern.description: Both adapters implement the same AuthAdapter interface. The app wires one at startup via an env flag or build config.

implementations.description: Implements AuthAdapter using better-auth endpoints. Uses cookie-less Bearer token flow for React Native.
Implements AuthAdapter for a custom backend. Endpoint paths configurable via env.

wiring.description: Exports the active adapter based on EXPO_PUBLIC_AUTH_ADAPTER env var ('better-auth' | 'custom'). Default is 'better-auth'.

cold_start: 4. If no token: clearAuth() → splash hidden → AuthGate redirects to sign-in
5. If token exists: check expiry from stored expiry timestamp (no network call needed if not expired)
6. If token not expired: call adapter.getSession(token)
7. getSession success: setAuth(user, token) → splash hidden → AuthGate redirects to app
9. Refresh success: store new tokens → setAuth → splash hidden
10. Refresh fail: secureStore.delete all keys → clearAuth() → splash hidden → redirect sign-in
11. expo-splash-screen.hideAsync() called only after auth state is resolved
foreground_resume: 3. If expired: silent refresh attempt in background
4. If refresh fails: set sessionExpiredModalVisible=true in useUIStore
5. SessionExpiredModal shown over current screen — user does not lose navigation context
6. User taps 'Sign in again' → modal closes → redirect to sign-in with router.replace
logout: 1. Call adapter.logout(token) — fire and forget, don't block UI on failure
7. router.replace('/(auth)/sign-in') — replace, not push, so back button cannot return to app

pages.name: SignUpScreen
pages.description: Registration screen with name, email, password fields

actions.name: navigateToSignIn
actions.description: Already have an account? Sign in

pages.name: ForgotPasswordScreen
pages.description: Enter email to receive OTP for password reset

actions.name: submitForgotPassword
actions.triggers: calls API then navigates to OTP screen

pages.name: OtpScreen
pages.description: Enter 6-digit OTP code sent to email. Auto-advance on fill. Resend timer.

actions.name: verifyOtp
actions.description: Verify OTP and get reset token
actions.triggers: calls API then navigates to reset password

pages.name: ResetPasswordScreen
pages.description: Set new password using reset token from OTP verification

actions.name: submitResetPassword
actions.triggers: calls API then redirects to sign-in

pages.name: SessionExpiredModal
pages.route: modal — rendered in app/_layout.tsx over current screen
pages.description: Non-navigating modal overlay shown when token expires mid-session. Does not lose current screen context.

actions.name: signInAgain
actions.description: Close modal and redirect to sign-in

pages.name: AccountLockedScreen
pages.description: Shown when backend returns account locked/suspended error code. Non-dismissable. Shows support contact.

actions.name: contactSupport
actions.description: Open email client or support URL

forms.success_message: Check your email for the OTP code
forms.error_message: Could not send reset email. Try again.

forms.success_message: Password reset successfully. Please sign in.
forms.error_message: Reset failed. Your link may have expired.

data_flow: 5. On success: secureStore.set(TOKEN_KEY, token), secureStore.set(REFRESH_TOKEN_KEY), secureStore.set(TOKEN_EXPIRY_KEY)
7. AuthGate detects session and redirects to /(app)/
8. On token expiry mid-session: AppState listener fires, refresh attempted, on fail sessionExpiredModalVisible=true
9. SessionExpiredModal shown, user taps sign in again, router.replace sign-in

acceptance_criteria.given: A new user opens the app
acceptance_criteria.when: They tap sign up from the sign-in screen
acceptance_criteria.then: They are navigated to sign-up screen and can register successfully

acceptance_criteria.given: A user has forgotten their password
acceptance_criteria.when: They complete forgot password → OTP → reset password flow
acceptance_criteria.then: Their password is updated and they are redirected to sign-in

acceptance_criteria.given: A user's token expires while they are on the home screen
acceptance_criteria.when: The app detects expiry on foreground resume
acceptance_criteria.then: SessionExpiredModal appears over the current screen without losing navigation context

acceptance_criteria.given: The backend returns an account locked error on sign-in
acceptance_criteria.when: The sign-in mutation receives the error
acceptance_criteria.then: User is redirected to AccountLockedScreen, not shown a generic error
All auth calls use custom.adapter.ts — no better-auth code executes
SecureStore is cleared, queryClient cache is cleared, user lands on sign-in, back button cannot return to app screens

deep_link_auth_recovery.description: User taps a push notification or shared link while unauthenticated. App must redirect to sign-in, then after successful auth, navigate to the original intended destination. This is the most common deep link edge case.

implementation.step_1: In (auth)/_layout.tsx, before redirecting to sign-in, read the incoming URL via Linking.getInitialURL() or expo-router useURL()
implementation.step_2: Store the intended route in useUIStore as pendingDeepLink: string | null
implementation.step_3: After successful sign-in in mutations.ts, check useUIStore.pendingDeepLink
implementation.step_4: If pendingDeepLink exists: router.replace(pendingDeepLink), then clear pendingDeepLink in store
implementation.step_5: If no pendingDeepLink: router.replace('/(app)/) as normal

deep_link_auth_recovery.ui_store_field: pendingDeepLink: string | null — add to src/store/slices/ui.slice.ts

multi_session_conflict.description: Backend detects the same account authenticated on another device and invalidates the current session. The API returns 401 with a specific error code (e.g. SESSION_INVALIDATED_ELSEWHERE).

implementation.detection: tRPC onError / fetch interceptor checks for SESSION_INVALIDATED_ELSEWHERE error code
implementation.action: Set sessionExpiredModalVisible=true in useUIStore with a specific message: 'Your account was signed in on another device'
implementation.modal: SessionExpiredModal already built in this MVP — pass the message as a prop, it handles both expiry and conflict cases

android_back_handler.description: Android hardware back button requires explicit handling on auth screens and protected screens to prevent navigation into wrong states.
rules: sign-in screen: BackHandler.exitApp() — back exits the app, does not navigate anywhere
OTP screen: back goes to forgot-password screen — normal navigation
reset-password screen: back goes to OTP screen — normal navigation
After logout: router.replace() ensures back stack is cleared — no BackHandler needed if replace is used correctly
android_back_handler.implementation: Use useFocusEffect + BackHandler.addEventListener('hardwareBackPress', handler) in each screen that needs custom back behavior. Return true from handler to prevent default.

shared_ui_components.description: These components are built in MVP 2 and used across all subsequent MVPs. Every data screen must use all three states.

files_to_create.description: Empty state component. Props: title, description, ctaLabel, onCta, icon (optional). Used when a query returns empty data.
files_to_create.usage_rule: Every list/feed screen must render EmptyState when data.length === 0

files_to_create.description: Error state component. Props: title, description, onRetry. Used when a query fails. Retry button calls refetch().
files_to_create.usage_rule: Every data screen must render ErrorState when isError === true, passing refetch as onRetry

files_to_create.description: Inline banner shown when some data loaded but part of a screen failed. Non-blocking — shows above content. Props: message, onRetry.
files_to_create.usage_rule: Used when a screen has multiple queries and only some fail — show what succeeded, banner what failed

per_screen_state_contract.description: Every screen that fetches data must handle all four states. No exceptions.
states: isError → render ErrorState with refetch as onRetry
data.length === 0 → render EmptyState with relevant CTA

edge_cases: OTP resend timer must persist through keyboard open/close — use useRef for timer, not useState
On Android, back button on sign-in screen should exit the app, not navigate back into app screens — handle with BackHandler.exitApp()
confirmPassword validation must use Zod refine() with superRefine for cross-field validation — not just .min()
Token expiry check on foreground resume must debounce — rapid background/foreground cycles could trigger multiple refresh calls
If adapter.logout() fails network call, still clear local state — never block logout on network failure
Account locked screen must not show back button — user cannot bypass it by navigating back
pendingDeepLink in UIStore must be cleared after consumption — uncleaned deep link causes redirect loop on next auth
out_of_scope: Social auth (Google, Apple) — not in template scope
Email verification on sign-up (separate from forgot password OTP) — add only if backend requires it
